name: dep_add_transitive_cycle_rejected
purpose: Three-node transitive blocks cycle A->B->C->A rejected on the closing edge.
pins: First two adds succeed; closing edge dep-3->dep-1 errors 'adding dependency would create a cycle'; exit 1.

name: dep_bare_no_args_help
purpose: Bare 'bd dep' with no args and no --blocks prints help and exits 0.
pins: Prints command help to stdout; exit 0 (no error). Help text content may differ across implementations; assert exit 0 and that it is not an error envelope.

name: dep_conditional_blocks_close_unblocks
purpose: conditional-blocks behaves like blocks for readiness: dependent blocked until target closed.
pins: dep-2 blocked by conditional-blocks while dep-1 open (blocked_by=[dep-1]); after closing dep-1, dep-2 ready and blocked empty.

name: dep_cycles_detected_via_no_cycle_check
purpose: Bypass per-edge cycle check with --no-cycle-check to plant a 2-node blocks cycle, then dep cycles reports it.
pins: Both adds succeed (single-edge add path honors --no-cycle-check); dep cycles --json returns a non-empty array of cycles containing {dep-1,dep-2}. Cycle member ordering compared as multiset.

name: dep_cycles_none
purpose: dep cycles on an acyclic graph returns empty array.
pins: Returns [] (no cycles); exit 0. With --no-cycle-check we could create one, but normal adds prevent cycles so an acyclic graph reports none.

name: dep_invalid_type
purpose: bd dep add with an unknown dependency --type fails with the valid-types list, exit 1.
pins: create_deps.go / dep.go: 'unknown dependency type %q; valid types: %s' via HandleErrorRespectJSON. Verify dep add uses --type flag name. exit 1.
notes: Check exact flag (dep add may take type as positional or --type). Grounds the dep-type validation message.

name: dep_list_batch_down_flat_array_shape
purpose: dep list with MULTIPLE ids in down direction uses the batch path returning a FLAT []Dependency array (issue_id/depends_on_id/type), distinct from the single-issue IssueWithDependencyMetadata shape.
pins: Batch down returns flat Dependency records {issue_id, depends_on_id, type} for both dep-1->dep-3(blocks) and dep-2->dep-3(related); multiset compare; this is a DIFFERENT JSON shape than the single-id path.

name: dep_list_batch_partial_unresolvable
purpose: Batch dep list with one unresolvable id warns to stderr (skipped) and returns deps for the resolvable ids; if none resolve, returns [].
pins: Stderr warning 'warning: ... dep-999 ... (skipped)'; stdout JSON includes dep-1->dep-2 edge; exit 0 (batch mode tolerates unresolved).

name: dep_list_down_default_single
purpose: dep list (default direction down) for a single issue returns IssueWithDependencyMetadata array of its dependencies.
pins: Single-issue down returns []IssueWithDependencyMetadata with both targets dep-2(blocks) and dep-3(related); each carries full issue fields + dependency_type. Compared as multiset.

name: dep_list_empty_no_deps
purpose: dep list for an issue with no dependencies returns an empty JSON array (down) and empty for up.
pins: Both down and up return [] (empty array, not null) with exit 0.

name: dep_list_no_args_error
purpose: dep list with no issue id args fails MinimumNArgs(1).
pins: cobra MinimumNArgs(1) error; nonzero exit.

name: dep_list_notfound_single
purpose: Single-issue dep list on a nonexistent id is a hard error (not skipped).
pins: Error 'resolving dep-999' / 'no issue found: dep-999'; exit 1; JSON error envelope on stdout.

name: dep_list_type_filter
purpose: dep list --type filters to a single dependency type.
pins: -t blocks returns only the dep-2 edge; -t related returns only the dep-3 edge; non-matching types excluded.

name: dep_list_up_dependents
purpose: dep list --direction=up returns dependents (issues that depend on the target) as IssueWithDependencyMetadata.
pins: up returns {dep-2(blocks), dep-3(related)} as dependents; multiset compare; dependency_type reflects the edge each dependent uses.

name: dep_multi_blocker_partial_close
purpose: Multi-blocker readiness: a dependent with two open blockers stays blocked until BOTH are closed.
pins: Two blockers: blocked dep-3 blocked_by_count=2 [dep-1,dep-2]; after closing dep-1, dep-3 still blocked blocked_by_count=1 [dep-2] and NOT ready; after closing dep-2, dep-3 becomes ready. blocked_by compared as multiset.

name: dep_parent_child_blocked_inheritance
purpose: parent-child: a child inherits blocked state when its parent is blocked, and is ready when the parent is unblocked (parent-child alone does not block the child directly).
pins: Before blocker: child dep-2.1 and parent dep-2 both ready (parent-child alone doesn't block child). After parent dep-2 blocked by dep-1: parent blocked AND child inherits blocked; ready={dep-1}; blocked includes dep-2 (blocked_by dep-1) and dep-2.1 (inherited, blocked_by=[dep-2]).

name: dep_remove_arg_count_error
purpose: dep remove requires exactly 2 args; fewer/more is a cobra usage error.
pins: ExactArgs(2) violated -> usage/arg error to stderr; nonzero exit (cobra arg validation).

name: dep_remove_existing_unblocks
purpose: Removing a blocks edge unblocks the dependent and returns it to ready.
pins: remove JSON = {status:removed, issue_id:dep-2, depends_on_id:dep-1}; after remove ready={dep-1,dep-2}, blocked empty, dep list dep-2 shows no dependencies.

name: dep_remove_nonexistent_noop
purpose: Removing a dependency that does not exist between two real issues is a silent no-op success (exit 0).
pins: No edge exists; command succeeds with {status:removed, issue_id:dep-1, depends_on_id:dep-2}; exit 0; no error.

name: dep_remove_rm_alias
purpose: The 'rm' alias behaves identically to 'remove'.
pins: rm removes the edge identically; dep list dep-2 shows no dependencies afterward.

name: dep_remove_then_retype_succeeds
purpose: Full retype workflow: remove the existing typed edge then re-add with a new type succeeds (complements the retype-reject scenario).
pins: After remove+re-add, dep list dep-1 shows a single related edge to dep-2 (blocks gone); dep-1 no longer blocked.

name: dep_self_dependency_json
purpose: bd dep add <id> <id> --json fails 'cannot add self-dependency: <id> cannot depend on itself' as stdout JSON envelope, exit 1.
pins: dep.go add path -> HandleErrorRespectJSON wrapping 'cannot add self-dependency: %s cannot depend on itself'. json -> stdout. Confirmed empirically.
notes: Distinct from cycle_reject (existing). Self-loop is a special-cased message.

name: dep_top_level_blocks_requires_one_arg
purpose: Top-level dep --blocks with zero positional args errors (needs exactly one blocker id).
pins: Error '--blocks requires exactly one issue ID argument'; exit 1.

name: dep_top_level_blocks_shorthand
purpose: Top-level 'bd dep <blocker> --blocks <blocked>' shorthand: blocker blocks blocked. Pin its DISTINCT JSON shape (blocker_id/blocked_id, not issue_id/depends_on_id).
pins: Top-level --blocks JSON = {status:added, blocker_id:dep-1, blocked_id:dep-2, type:blocks} (different keys than 'dep add'); edge stored as dep-2 depends-on dep-1; dep-2 blocked, dep-1 ready.

name: dep_tree_down_default
purpose: dep tree (default down) JSON returns TreeNode array of dependencies with edge_from_parent and depth.
pins: TreeNode array rooted at dep-1 (depth 0) with dep-2 (depth1, edge_from_parent=blocks) and dep-3 (depth2, blocks); truncated=false. Multiset compare of nodes.

name: dep_tree_empty
purpose: dep tree for an issue with no deps returns empty JSON array.
pins: Empty array [] (not null); exit 0.

name: dep_tree_invalid_direction
purpose: dep tree with an invalid --direction value errors.
pins: Error "--direction must be 'down', 'up', or 'both'"; exit 1.

name: dep_tree_maxdepth_zero_invalid
purpose: dep tree --max-depth 0 is rejected (must be >= 1).
pins: Error '--max-depth must be >= 1'; exit 1.

name: dep_tree_status_filter
purpose: dep tree --status filters to nodes of a given status while keeping ancestors; closing a leaf changes which nodes appear.
pins: Status filter 'closed' keeps dep-3 plus its ancestor chain (dep-1); dep-2 (open) excluded. Deterministic IDs make node set byte-comparable.

name: dep_tree_up_direction
purpose: dep tree --direction=up shows dependents tree.
pins: Up tree rooted at dep-1 includes dep-2 as a dependent node; structure mirrors the down case inverted.

name: dep_waits_for_all_children_gate
purpose: waits-for (default all-children) gate: waiter blocked while spawner has an open parent-child child; unblocks when all children closed.
pins: While dep-2.1 open: dep-1 blocked (waits-for gate, blocked_by=[dep-2]); after closing the only child, dep-1 returns to ready. Pins all-children gate semantics.

name: dep_waits_for_no_children_not_blocked
purpose: Edge case: a waits-for gate where the spawner has NO parent-child children does NOT block the waiter (outer EXISTS false).
pins: With zero spawned children, dep-1 is NOT blocked: ready contains dep-1; blocked empty. Confirms waits-for gates only on existing open children.

name: ephemeral_deferred_status_and_ready_hidden
purpose: Pin that --defer on an ephemeral create yields status=deferred AND the bead is hidden from default ready (both by defer and by ephemeral).
pins: create.go:741-744 future defer -> Status=deferred. ready.go:98 ephemeral filter + ready.go:125 defer_until>now both exclude it; even --include-ephemeral excludes it (still deferred, IncludeDeferred unset).

name: format_json_alias
purpose: bd version --format json behaves identically to --json (the hidden --format alias), exit 0.
pins: main.go PersistentPreRunE: if --format changed and EqualFold(format,'json') -> jsonOutput=true. version.go then emits the json shape. Pins the --format=json desire-path alias.
notes: Non-byte-diffable version values, but confirms --format json == --json routing.

name: global_requires_shared_server
purpose: bd --global <cmd> in non-shared-server mode fails '--global requires shared-server mode (...)', exit 1.
pins: main.go: globalFlag && !IsSharedServerMode() -> HandleError('--global requires shared-server mode (set BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 or dolt.shared-server: true in config.yaml)'). exit 1. Plain HandleError -> stderr even with --json.
notes: Embedded init is not shared-server, so --global always trips here. stderr exit 1.

name: init_already_initialized
purpose: Running bd init twice fails: second init aborts with the 'workspace is already initialized' guidance, exit 1, no data destroyed.
pins: init.go checkExistingBeadsData -> alreadyInitialized() returns errWorkspaceAlreadyInitialized; without --init-if-missing it is fatal via 'fmt.Errorf(%v)' -> exit 1. Message includes 'This workspace is already initialized.' (embedded-mode variant lists the dolt path + reinit-local guidance).
notes: Message embeds the absolute embeddeddolt path (workspace-specific) and ui.RenderWarn ANSI; harness must normalize the path and strip color. Pin the stable substring 'This workspace is already initialized.' and exit 1.

name: init_backend_sqlite_removed
purpose: bd init --backend=sqlite prints the 'SQLite backend has been removed' deprecation block to stderr and fails '--backend=sqlite is no longer supported', exit 1.
pins: init.go lines 217-225: multi-line stderr notice + return fmt.Errorf('--backend=sqlite is no longer supported'). exit 1.
notes: Notice uses ui.RenderWarn ANSI; normalize color. The fatal error line is stable. Pin the substring 'no longer supported'.

name: init_if_missing_idempotent
purpose: bd init --init-if-missing on an already-initialized workspace SKIPS and exits 0 with 'Skipping init: workspace already initialized.' on stderr.
pins: init.go: initIfMissing && errors.Is(err, errWorkspaceAlreadyInitialized) -> prints 'Skipping init: workspace already initialized.' (unless --quiet) and returns nil (exit 0).
notes: Pins the idempotent-skip contract and its exit 0. Message is stable (no path).

name: init_if_missing_prefix_mismatch
purpose: bd init --init-if-missing with a DIFFERENT --prefix than the existing workspace fails 'workspace already initialized as database ... but --prefix ... was requested', exit 1.
pins: init.go: initIfMissingPrefixMismatch -> fmt.Errorf('workspace already initialized as database %q, but --prefix %q was requested...'). Guards against silently reusing a mismatched workspace. exit 1.
notes: Message embeds the existing database name (derived from first prefix, deterministic) — should be byte-stable if prefix is fixed. Verify db-name derivation matches between bd and the candidate.

name: init_invalid_database_name
purpose: bd init --database with an invalid identifier fails 'invalid database name "<x>": ...', exit 1.
pins: init.go lines 231-234 dolt.ValidateDatabaseName -> fmt.Errorf('invalid database name %q: %v'). Validated early before side effects. exit 1.
notes: The %v from ValidateDatabaseName may have backend-specific wording; pin the 'invalid database name' prefix and that it fails before FS changes. Verify exact ValidateDatabaseName text.

name: init_invalid_role
purpose: bd init --role bogus fails 'invalid --role "bogus": must be "maintainer" or "contributor"', exit 1.
pins: init.go lines 242-248 return fmt.Errorf('invalid --role %q: must be "maintainer" or "contributor"'). exit 1.
notes: Validated before side effects. Stable message.

name: init_json_success_shape
purpose: bd init --json --non-interactive emits a machine-readable success object on stdout (or, if init does not support --json, falls back to human) — establish which.
pins: init.go RunE largely prints human progress lines to stdout/stderr; verify whether --json produces a structured envelope or is ignored. Grounds the output-mode contract for init.
notes: init output includes repo/clone IDs and paths (non-reproducible). Mark non-deterministic; the scenario's value is confirming WHICH stream/shape init uses under --json so the candidate matches routing, not bytes.

name: init_noninteractive_rejects_contributor
purpose: bd init --non-interactive --contributor fails '--contributor requires interactive prompts and cannot be used with --non-interactive', exit 1.
pins: init.go line 253 return fmt.Errorf('--contributor requires interactive prompts and cannot be used with --non-interactive'). Sibling: --team. exit 1.
notes: Pins the non-interactive guard family. Add a --team sibling step/scenario.

name: init_proxied_server_not_implemented
purpose: bd init --proxied-server fails '--proxied-server is not yet implemented', exit 1.
pins: init.go line 139 return fmt.Errorf('--proxied-server is not yet implemented'). Short-circuits before any FS work. exit 1.
notes: Stable. Confirms proxied-server mode is unreachable (relevant to the FatalError telemetry note).

name: init_unknown_backend
purpose: bd init --backend=foo fails 'unknown backend "foo": only "dolt" is supported', exit 1.
pins: init.go line 227 return fmt.Errorf('unknown backend %q: only "dolt" is supported'). Plain RunE error -> main() prints 'Error: <msg>' to stderr (SilenceErrors path). exit 1.
notes: Pure validation, no side effects, stable message.

name: json_envelope_tripwire_list
purpose: A read command's --json output array is bare (flat) by default and wrapped in {schema_version,data:[...]} under BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1.
pins: output.go wrapWithSchemaVersion: slices/arrays returned bare in flat mode (no schema_version injected into a list); under envelope mode wrapped as {schema_version, data:[...]}. Run the list step twice (with and without env) or as paired scenarios.
notes: Key tripwire for arrays: flat mode does NOT add schema_version to a top-level array (only to objects), but DOES add it to objects. The candidate must replicate this asymmetry. Created issue normalized (id fixed via --force).

name: list_all_shows_closed
purpose: `--all` overrides default filter and includes closed issues.
pins: --all skips ExcludeStatus block (list_filter.go:150 !allFlag) — closed la-2 appears alongside la-1.

name: list_assignee_filter
purpose: `--assignee bob` returns only issues assigned to bob.
pins: filter.Assignee -> assignee = ? (sqlbuild/filter.go:87-90); only as-1.

name: list_combined_type_status_label
purpose: Combining `-t task --status open -l backend` ANDs all three filters.
pins: All predicates AND in WHERE; only cm-1 satisfies type=task AND status=open AND label backend.

name: list_completely_empty_db
purpose: `bd list --json` against a DB with no matching durable issues returns [] exit 0.
pins: Empty workspace yields [] exit 0; verifies the candidate empty-list shape parity with zero issues created.

name: list_created_after_far_future_empty
purpose: `--created-after <far-future date>` returns empty (boundary: nothing created after).
pins: CreatedAfter -> created_at > ? excludes all -> [] exit 0. Absolute future date keeps it deterministic.

name: list_created_before_far_future_all
purpose: `--created-before <far-future date>` returns all issues (boundary: everything created before).
pins: CreatedBefore -> created_at < ? (sqlbuild/filter.go:204); far-future date includes all. Absolute date keeps it deterministic vs wall-clock.

name: list_date_invalid_format
purpose: An unparseable date for `--created-before` is rejected with exit 1.
pins: parseListTimeFlag -> 'parsing --created-before' error exit 1 (list_input.go:351-354).

name: list_default_durable_only_excludes_both_wisp_tiers
purpose: Pin that default `bd list` shows DURABLE ONLY and excludes BOTH no_history and ephemeral beads (SkipWisps default).
pins: list_filter.go:317-319 SkipWisps=true by default; search.go:37 returns issues table only. Set = {st-ld}; no_history and ephemeral both absent.

name: list_default_excludes_closed
purpose: Bare `bd list --json` returns only non-closed, non-pinned, durable issues; closed issue is hidden by default.
pins: Default status filter excludes closed (ExcludeStatus=[closed,pinned] in list_filter.go:150-158); li-2 must be absent, li-1 and li-3 present.

name: list_desc_contains
purpose: `--desc-contains` matches a substring of the description.
pins: DescriptionContains -> LOWER(description) LIKE (sqlbuild/filter.go:40-43); only dc-1.

name: list_digraph_format_preset
purpose: `--format digraph` emits 'IssueID DependsOnID' lines for in-set edges.
pins: presets['digraph']='{{.IssueID}} {{.DependsOnID}}' (list_output.go:103-104); prints 'dg-1 dg-2'.

name: list_dot_format_dependency_edges
purpose: `--format dot` emits a Graphviz digraph with nodes and only intra-set dependency edges.
pins: outputDotFormat prints 'digraph dependencies {' nodes for dt-1/dt-2 and a dt-1 -> dt-2 edge labeled blocks (list_output.go:24-94). No --json (format overrides). Edges only when both nodes in filtered set.

name: list_empty_description
purpose: `--empty-description` returns issues with no description.
pins: EmptyDescription -> (description IS NULL OR ='') (sqlbuild/filter.go:192-194); only ed-1.

name: list_empty_result_exit0
purpose: A filter matching nothing returns JSON [] with exit code 0 (empty is not an error).
pins: No bugs -> SearchIssuesWithCounts empty -> [] (list.go:538-540) exit 0.

name: list_exclude_label
purpose: `--exclude-label` removes issues bearing ANY of the given labels.
pins: ExcludeLabels -> id NOT IN (...) (sqlbuild/filter.go:154-161); el-2 absent, el-1 present.

name: list_exclude_type
purpose: `--exclude-type epic,chore` removes those types from results.
pins: ExcludeTypes -> issue_type NOT IN (...) (list_filter.go:270-277); only xt-1 returned.

name: list_format_bad_template
purpose: An invalid Go-template `--format` value is rejected with a parse error.
pins: template.Parse fails -> 'invalid format template' error exit 1 (list_output.go:113-117). Edge must exist so the template path is reached.

name: list_format_json_string_switches_json
purpose: `--format json` is treated as --json (sets jsonOutput, clears formatStr).
pins: list_input.go:116-119 EqualFold('json') -> jsonOutput=true, formatStr=''; output is the JSON array, not a template.

name: list_has_metadata_key
purpose: `--has-metadata-key key` returns issues that have the key set regardless of value.
pins: HasMetadataKey -> JSON_EXTRACT(metadata,?) IS NOT NULL; only hk-1.

name: list_id_filter_nonexistent_empty
purpose: `--id` with an unknown ID returns an empty set (no error).
pins: id IN (...) matches nothing -> JSON [] exit 0; not-found is not an error for --id.

name: list_id_filter_subset
purpose: `--id a,b` returns exactly the named issues regardless of order, ignoring others.
pins: filter.IDs -> id IN (...) (sqlbuild/filter.go:105-112); returns id-1 and id-3 only.

name: list_include_gates
purpose: Gate-typed issues are hidden by default but shown with `--include-gates`.
pins: Default adds ExcludeTypes 'gate' (list_filter.go:260-262); first list excludes gt-2, second includes it.

name: list_include_infra_all_merges_full_wisps_tier
purpose: Pin that `bd list --include-infra --all` merges the wisps tier so the SET becomes DURABLE + NO_HISTORY + EPHEMERAL.
pins: list_filter.go:317 keeps SkipWisps=false under --include-infra; search.go:48-76 merges wisps (ephemeral + no_history rows). Set = {st-lid, st-linh, st-lie}.

name: list_include_infra_shows_agents
purpose: Infra-typed beads (agent/role/message) are hidden by default and shown with `--include-infra`.
pins: Default ExcludeTypes adds infra types + SkipWisps (list_filter.go:264-268,317-319); if-1 hidden first, shown with --include-infra. NOTE: if 'agent' type creation is rejected in this build, this is non-reproducible — flagged deterministic false.

name: list_include_templates
purpose: Template molecules are excluded by default and shown with `--include-templates`.
pins: Default sets IsTemplate=false (list_filter.go:255-258); template hidden in first list, present in second. NOTE: template-create syntax may differ; deterministic flagged false because template setup/IDs may be non-reproducible.

name: list_label_and_semantics
purpose: Multiple `--label` flags require ALL labels (AND).
pins: Each label emits a separate id IN (SELECT...label=?) so it is AND (sqlbuild/filter.go:140-145); only lb-1 has both.

name: list_label_any_or_semantics
purpose: `--label-any` matches issues having AT LEAST ONE of the labels (OR).
pins: LabelsAny -> id IN (SELECT...label IN (...)) (sqlbuild/filter.go:146-153); ly-1+ly-2, not ly-3.

name: list_label_case_insensitive
purpose: Label filter is normalized (lowercased) so uppercase input matches lowercase stored label.
pins: utils.NormalizeLabels lowercases the filter (list_input.go:294); lc-1 matches.

name: list_label_pattern_glob
purpose: `--label-pattern 'tech-*'` matches glob over label names.
pins: LabelPattern glob; lg-1+lg-2 match, lg-3 excluded.

name: list_label_regex
purpose: `--label-regex 'tech-(debt|legacy)'` matches via regex.
pins: LabelRegex predicate; lr-1 matches, lr-2 does not.

name: list_limit_truncates_set
purpose: `--limit 2` returns at most 2 issues (SQL LIMIT) — boundary of result-set size.
pins: effectiveLimit truncates to 2 (list.go:534-537). Pin --sort id for a stable, deterministic subset (ln-1,ln-2) rather than relying on natural multiset order.

name: list_limit_zero_unlimited
purpose: `--limit 0` returns all matching issues with no cap.
pins: effectiveLimit=0 disables truncation (list.go:534 effectiveLimit>0 false); all three returned.

name: list_long_format_text
purpose: `--long` (no --json) emits the detailed multi-line text block; verifies the 'Found N issues' header and per-issue detail.
pins: longFormat path writes 'Found 1 issues' + formatIssueLong with Assignee/Description (list.go:633-638, list_format.go:86-125). Text output is env-sensitive (color/agent-mode); run with color disabled + non-agent. --flat avoids tree.

name: list_metadata_field_bad_format
purpose: `--metadata-field` without '=' is rejected with a key=value error.
pins: strings.Cut fails -> 'invalid --metadata-field: expected key=value' exit 1 (list_input.go:253-256, HandleErrorRespectJSON emits JSON to stdout under --json).

name: list_metadata_field_exact
purpose: `--metadata-field key=value` returns only issues whose metadata key equals value.
pins: MetadataFields -> JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(metadata,?))=? (sqlbuild AppendMetadataClauses); only mf-1.

name: list_metadata_multiple_fields_and
purpose: Repeated `--metadata-field` flags AND together.
pins: Multiple metadata fields each add an AND clause (sorted keys, AppendMetadataClauses); only ma-1 matches both.

name: list_mol_type_invalid
purpose: Invalid `--mol-type` is rejected with exit 1 listing valid values.
pins: MolType.IsValid fails -> 'invalid mol-type' exit 1 (list_input.go:199-205).

name: list_no_assignee_filter
purpose: `--no-assignee` returns only unassigned issues.
pins: NoAssignee -> (assignee IS NULL OR assignee='') (sqlbuild/filter.go:195-197); only na-1.

name: list_no_labels_filter
purpose: `--no-labels` returns only issues that have zero labels.
pins: NoLabels -> id NOT IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels) (sqlbuild/filter.go:162-164); only nl-1.

name: list_no_parent_filter
purpose: `--no-parent` returns only top-level issues (those with no parent-child dependency).
pins: NoParent -> id NOT IN (SELECT issue_id WHERE type='parent-child') (sqlbuild/filter.go:127-129); np-1+np-3, not np-2.

name: list_offset_negative_rejected
purpose: Negative `--offset` is rejected (exit 1) at input validation.
pins: list_input.go:326-328 '--offset must be >= 0' exit 1 (validation occurs before the proxied-server gate).

name: list_offset_rejected_without_proxied
purpose: `--offset N` is rejected in direct (non-proxied-server) mode with a specific error, exit 1.
pins: list.go:492-494 HandleError '--offset is only supported under --proxied-server' exit 1.

name: list_parent_and_no_parent_conflict
purpose: `--parent` and `--no-parent` together are mutually exclusive (exit 1).
pins: list_input.go:195-197 returns HandleError exit 1 before query runs.

name: list_parent_children_json
purpose: `--parent X` returns children of X (via parent-child dep) but NOT the parent itself.
pins: ParentID subquery on parent-child deps (sqlbuild/filter.go:122-126); returns pa-2,pa-3, excludes pa-1 and pa-4.

name: list_parent_no_children_empty_json
purpose: `--parent X --json` where X has no children returns empty array (not the 'has no children' message, which is pretty-mode only).
pins: JSON path runs SearchIssues filter (no tree); empty -> [] exit 0. The 'Issue X has no children' string is only in pretty path (list.go:585-588).

name: list_parent_nonexistent_json_empty
purpose: `--parent BOGUS --json` for a non-existent parent returns empty (JSON path does not validate parent existence).
pins: JSON/flat path never calls getHierarchicalChildren so missing-parent is NOT an error here; filter yields []; exit 0. (Tree path WOULD error.)

name: list_pinned_and_no_pinned_conflict
purpose: `--pinned` with `--no-pinned` is mutually exclusive (exit 1).
pins: list_input.go:181-183 returns HandleError exit 1.

name: list_pinned_only
purpose: `--pinned` shows only pinned issues; default list hides pinned.
pins: Default forces pinned=false (list_filter.go:250) hiding pp-2; --pinned sets pinned=true (list_filter.go:247) showing only pp-2. Verify pin via update --status pinned is supported.

name: list_positional_arg_rejected
purpose: `bd list foo` (positional) is rejected — list takes flags only.
pins: Args validator returns 'bd list does not accept positional arguments' (list.go:458) exit 1.

name: list_positional_known_flag_hint
purpose: `bd list ready` (a flag-name typed as positional) yields the special did-you-mean hint.
pins: knownListFlags maps 'ready' -> hint 'did you mean "--ready" or bd ready?' (list.go:454-456) exit 1.

name: list_priority_exact
purpose: `-p 0` returns only P0 issues.
pins: filter.Priority -> priority = ? (sqlbuild/filter.go:92-95); only pr-1.

name: list_priority_invalid

name: version_human_default

pins: version.go non-json branch: three printf variants depending on commit/branch presence. Always stdout, never stderr (absent PATH-dup warning).
notes: Version string + commit + branch differ between bd and the candidate builds; non-byte-diffable. Pin only the 'bd version ' prefix and the parenthesized shape.

name: version_json_flat_envelope
purpose: bd version --json default (flat) shape: object with build/version and injected schema_version=1, on stdout, exit 0.
pins: version.go: jsonOutput branch emits map{version,build} through outputJSON; output.go wrapWithSchemaVersion injects schema_version into the object. commit/branch keys omitted when empty (dev build).
notes: version/build/commit values are non-reproducible across the two binaries, so diff must normalize the values; the KEY SET and schema_version=1 are what is pinned. RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03 (W1.4): version identity is implementation-defined by definition — byte-parity retired when bts stopped impersonating bd. Shape (key set + schema_version=1) is pinned by bts-cli unit tests (version_payload tests in crates/bts-cli/src/main.rs). Golden deleted; bd's reference bytes preserved in git history. See docs/ORACLE_PROVENANCE.md.

name: version_json_v2_envelope_tripwire
purpose: BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1 wraps version --json in {schema_version, data:{...}} instead of flat. The v1-vs-v2 envelope tripwire.
pins: output.go jsonEnvelopeEnabled() reads BD_JSON_ENVELOPE==1; wrapWithSchemaVersion returns {schema_version:1, data:v}. data has no schema_version inside.
notes: Harness must run this step with env BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1. Compare against real bd with the same env. Normalize version/build values. RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03 (W1.4): re-scoped with version_json_flat_envelope (identity is implementation-defined). This golden never actually pinned the v2 envelope: run_scenario sets only BEADS_TEST_MODE=1 and has no per-scenario env, so BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1 was never set at capture — the captured bytes were the flat envelope. Golden deleted; shape pinned by bts-cli version_payload unit tests. See docs/ORACLE_PROVENANCE.md.

name: version_root_flag_ignores_json

notes: Pins a real behavioral contract (flag vs subcommand). Version value non-reproducible.

name: version_short_flags_V_and_long

notes: Second variant ['--version'] should be a sibling scenario or extra step. Output is the bare two-field form regardless of --json. Non-byte-diffable version value.
